chapter_tutorial: ^How far did I fall? And where’s Alice? I hope she isn’t hurt…? Alice? Alice!! Alice, answer me, where are you!?
^Oh, thank goodness, I thought I’d lost you. Come here so I can see you, please?
^Press the space bar to switch perspectives…
^I’ll be the judge of that, thank you.
^Oh my god, you don’t need to check me over for injuries, I’m fine! Who are you, Mom?
^I’ll be just as grey as her within a year if you keep running off like you just did, that’s for sure.
^Care to tell me where we are? And why?
^I saw a rabbit! Con, it had a vest on! And– and a pocketwatch! And so I followed it, of course. Now we’re at the bottom of a rabbit hole, I think. Isn’t it amazing?
^A rabbit. A rabbit. You jumped down a rabbit hole, chasing a vest-wearing rabbit.
^…Okay. Sure. Alright. That makes no sense. I tripped on the rabbit hole chasing you across a field, cracked my skull open on a rock, and now I’m dead. Right?
^No, silly! We’re just in a huge, otherworldly lobby at the bottom of an unfathomably deep rabbit hole. Look, they even have refreshments, how nice!
^Ooh, mystery tea, my favourite! It’s even asking me to drink it!
^Already enamoured with the world of Wonderland, Alice reaches enthusiastically for the bottle.
^Ohh, no you don’t. Alice, if you drink “mystery tea” again, I am disowning you. Put it down.
^Okay but the last mystery tea incident was just riverwater, I was only sick for like a day. This is real mystery tea! The genuine article!
^I said put it down. You don’t know what’s in that, it could be poison for all you know.
^Alice gulps down the ‘Drink Me’ labelled bottle, looking entirely too self-satisfied.

c-0: ^Alice, no! Are you stupid!? Spit it out, now!
^Am I st–? Constance, you are such a jerk, I’m fine! Ugh, let go of me!
^…I didn’t mean it like that, I was just–
^A tension weighs in the air… The sisters’ relationship is damaged.
c-1: ^Alice– Alice, look at me. Do you feel well? Do you feel sick? Are you okay?
^Am I okay–? I’m– I’m good, Constance, don’t worry. See? Totally fine!
^I don’t feel sick at all! I promise, okay?
^I won’t do it again, alright? Don’t look so scared.
^A sense of relief fills the air. The sisters’ relationship improves.

after_choice: ^Alright, enough of that! Let’s go explore!
^Don’t think I don’t know what you’re doing.
tutorial_puzzle_chapter: ^Oh come on, let’s just focus on all the cool things in here! Stop worrying so much and take a look around!
^Hm… a door…? It looks like our only way forward, and thereby the only way out of this strange place…
^Well that solves that! First order of business, let’s find ourselves a key!
^Try looking around the room! There are puzzles to be found here in Wonderland… Maybe they’ll offer a way out.
^Keep an eye on both sisters’ perspectives. Wonderland shows itself in different ways to different people.
tutorial_puzzle_complete: ^Woah, look at that! Come on, Con, let’s go, let’s go!
^Skipping out through the door, Alice drags her sister along to their next destination…
MeetCatPuzzle: ^A cat, oh my gosh it’s a talking cat!!!
^How rude. I'm not just any talking cat. I'm quite the step up from your average house pet.
^His grin feels a little toothy but Alice doesn't seem to mind. She looks enamored by what she sees.
^Me? Just your average run of the mill neighbor, nothing new to see. Though if it’s a name you are seeking, the Cheshire Cat is just fine.
^Cheshire Cat? That’s an odd name… but I like it. Nice to meet you!
^Alice’s enthusiasm is quite infectious. It’s hard not to smile around her. The cat gives her another cheeky grin.
^So now then, who might you be deary?
^Come on, enough of this, we have places to be so lets get going. Sorry to bother you again.
^Oh my what’s the rush, I was hoping we could stay and have a quick little chat!
^No need, we have no interest in chatting.
^Speak for yourself, I’ve got plenty of questions-
^No you don’t, you and I are leaving.
^So soon? We haven’t even sat down for tea yet? It’s quite rude don’t you think?
^Perhaps poor Constance feels uneasy around this new creature. It’s quite the intimidating foe, sometimes people get scared without it even trying.
^Not now, please just not now Alice.
^Constance tugs her sister down the road they came from, her legs rushing forward, ears trying to drown out Alice’s pleas to stop and talk to the Cat.
^The Cat, seemingly unaware of its unwantedness simply tags along, gently trotting along the path with the girls. This only seems to agitate poor Constance.
^Oh come now, it’s no fun if you ignore me the whole time. I don’t want to talk to your backs for the rest of the walk.
^Then perhaps this is a sign you need to find a path of your own, We’re quite busy after all.
^Oh yes, of course I’m sure you're very busy. It must be difficult, walking up in a strange world, unsure what’s up and what’s down.
^He watches in delight as this causes both girls to freeze in their path, eyes wide seemingly spooked by what they just heard.
^Well I’ve certainly never seen you here before, and I know everybody, so that means you're new.
^That’s awesome! You know everyone here in Wonderland?
^Of course I do. There isn't a person here I don't know. I'm quite sought out here.
^So it seems I’ve finally peaked your interest! How wonderful.
^So what is this place? Where is everyone? Are there more people like you here? Are-
^My aren’t we eager. Well it's all in good fun so I suppose there's no harm in letting you know this once.
^The cat appears thrilled by this latest discovery. Alice seems puzzled by this revelation.
^I’m more than happy to answer your questions my dear I only ask that you give me something in return for my troubles.
^Something strange seems to cross Constance's face, her hand tightening on her sisters. She looks ready to bolt.
^No thank you, I’m sure we’ll find our way to a village or somewhere with more people.
^Really? You think you can make it out of these enchanted woods without a guide? I can’t tell if you’re foolish or brave.
^The cat lets out a cackle that feels strange and pitchy, the air shifts uncomfortably around them.
^You mentioned that people do exist here. I’m sure we’ll stumble upon them soon. So thank you for your concern however we’re okay for now.
^Constance begins to pull her sister forward, intending to put more distance between them and the strange creature.
^What a shame… and here I thought you’d be interested in what I wanted to tell you. After all, it's not often you meet someone associated with our Queen.
^This causes both girls to freeze and turn again. Alice looks shocked and excited while Constance holds a look between relief and suspicion.

c-0: ^Oh my god Alice no!!! Not again
^Exhausted but amused by her sister's lively attitude Constance shelves her worry to chase after her quickly.
c-1: ^ALICE I SWEAR TO GOD IF YOU LEAD US DOWN ANOTHER RABBIT HOLE I WILL NOT BE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE THINGS I SAY.
^Oh my god Con you worry way too much, it’s fine!
^I don’t feel sick at all! I promise, okay?
^…Why can’t I have one day, just one normal day to myself?
^Frustrated by her sister's carelessness Constance quickly gives chase to her wayward sibling.

after_choice_3: ^The game of cat and mouse is ready to begin. A strange smile that had appeared in the distance materializes once more, a sharp looking feline now attached to that sporting grin.
MeetCat: ^The girls wander outside the room, a vast and lush forward stretches on for what seems like forever.
^Realizing there’s nowhere else to go, the girls tread carefully down the pathway. They walk until their feet feel sore and everything starts to ache.
^I cannot walk a single step further, my feet hurt and my head’s starting to spin.
^Constance feels like her sister looks like a wilted flower, she wisely chooses not to voice this aloud in hopes of keeping Alice calm.
^I don’t like it but I suppose we can take a quick break.
^But only a small one, five minutes that’s it.
^Alice nods and happily parks herself down on the path, much to Constance’s disgust. Silence fills the area around them.
^It’s unsettling, Constance wonders why she can’t hear the wildlife here despite it being midday.
^Maybe it’s cause of the heat? All the creatures went into hibernation?
^Constance looks at her sister in confusion.
^The animals, you said that it was weird you couldn’t hear the wildlife right?
^Alice I didn’t say that out loud???
^Really? Oh... it must have been the voices.
^yeahhh it sounds pretty nice though, so don’t worry about it.
^Yeah no thank you, I’ll worry however I please. Knowing you whatever this is will be plenty of trouble.
^Constance lets out a little sigh, something that makes Alice giggle softly.
^Oh but look here something sits in the distance, just beyond the tree?
^Okay hang on, let’s think this through before we jump head first-
^Alice takes off after the mysterious thing.

c-0: ^Oh my god Alice no!!! Not again
^Exhausted but amused by her sister's lively attitude Constance shelves her worry to chase after her quickly.
c-1: ^ALICE I SWEAR TO GOD IF YOU LEAD US DOWN ANOTHER RABBIT HOLE I WILL NOT BE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE THINGS I SAY.
^Oh my god Con you worry way too much, it’s fine!
^I don’t feel sick at all! I promise, okay?
^…Why can’t I have one day, just one normal day to myself?
^Frustrated by her sister's carelessness Constance quickly gives chase to her wayward sibling.

after_choice_2: ^The game of cat and mouse is ready to begin. A strange smile that had appeared in the distance materializes once more, a sharp looking feline now attached to that sporting grin.
